steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank completes a “disproving the common misconception of Native art as ______” framing, so the correct word must be the opposite of what the passage then describes: artists working in traditional tribal styles, European modernist traditions, abstract expressionism, and blended styles — a wide range of approaches.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — uncontroversial: Means not causing dispute; the passage never implies anyone considers Native art controversial or non-controversial — the misconception is about stylistic sameness, not controversy. ✗
  • Choice B — individualistic: Means emphasizing personal uniqueness; a range of individual styles would actually support “individualistic,” so this word cannot be the misconception that the diversity disproves. ✗
  • Choice C — theoretical: Means concerned with abstract ideas rather than practice; the text says nothing about people mistakenly viewing Native art as theoretical, and the listed artistic traditions are practical styles, not theoretical stances. ✗
  • Choice D — homogeneous: Means uniform or all the same; this is exactly the misconception disproved by listing four distinct traditions, showing Native art is stylistically diverse, not uniform. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
